title: chrysanthemum kokanicum
intro: Chrysanthemum kokanicum, also known as Indian chamomile or garland flower, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids. Chrysanthemum kokanicum has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, colds, and digestive issues, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation. The herbal preparations of Chrysanthemum kokanicum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to alleviate symptoms associated with stress, anxiety, and insomnia.
